source_jobs.title: Head/Director of Product (AI Insights & Automation)
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE ARE BUILDING

Mimica's mission is to empower enterprises, teams, and individuals to reclaim their most precious resource — time and work more efficiently, with greater purpose and impact.

Our AI-powered task mining observes employee actions across the desktop and categorizes them into detailed process maps. Mimica’s process intelligence highlights inefficiencies, prioritizes improvements based on ROI, recommends the optimal technology for automation (RPA, intelligent document processing, GenAI), and provides a blueprint for building new automations and transforming work.

Visit our Career Page https://www.mimica.ai/careers to learn more.




Mission

You will be the first Head of Product at Mimica, leading the product function through its next major growth phase.

This role is about leading a product organisation as it scales with ambition and intention. You will own the macro strategy, our market positioning and product roadmap, raise the bar on product management, and introduce the structures, principles, and ways of working that help teams make better decisions and move faster with confidence.

You’ll work closely with the CEO, CTO, Product Managers, Designers, and leaders across Engineering, Machine Learning, and Go-to-Market functions to set and align on strategy, prioritise effectively, and ensure we continue building products that deliver clear customer and business value.

We believe the most important product decisions happen before a line of code is written. We're building the discovery muscle: understanding why customers work the way they do, what they're actually trying to accomplish, and where our technology can create outcomes they couldn't get elsewhere. Your impact as Head of Product will be to drive that shift and embed that understanding at the company level.

It’s a rare opportunity to take on a foundational product leadership role at a well-funded AI scale-up with deep technical expertise, proprietary AI capabilities, and meaningful growth opportunities.





Part of your day-to-day

 1. Define, own, and champion the global product strategy and macro-level roadmap, including identifying gaps, shaping priorities, and long-term vision in partnership with the C-suite.

 2. Document and communicate the product vision and strategy to improve clarity and alignment, and turn informal decisions into clear direction.

 3. Mentor and develop Product Managers and Designers, and define career ladders and growth paths to assure the progression and upskilling of our team.

 4. Support and unblock your team when needed to maintain a healthy, sustainable, and high-performing team.

 5. Improve the operating model of the product function: prioritise and plan rituals; bring customer, market, and competitive insights earlier in the decision-making process and inform investment decisions.

 6. Align stakeholders and build consensus when priorities or opinions conflict.

 7. Handle executive and customer escalations and protect team focus when leadership or Sales pressure disrupts the roadmap.

 8. Ensure we retain a culture of strong ownership, transparency, and initiative while assuring business goals are met.

 9. Forecast future team capacity needs, and partner with the People team to hire top-tier talent to build an effective and well-balanced Product organisation.



Who are we looking for

 1.  A product leader who has operated as Head of Product, Director of Product, or VP of Product in a comparable Scale-up environment.

 2.  Experience building and managing one of the following technical Enterprise products or categories: data-heavy, process optimisation, AI, or automation products.

 3.  A strong people leader with a track record of coaching and mentoring both Product Managers and Product Designers.

 4.  Strong stakeholder management skills, particularly driving alignment between senior leaders in Engineering and Commercial functions.

 5.  Experience in building products powered by in-house developed ML/AI

source_jobs.title: Staff/Lead Platform/DevOps Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE ARE BUILDING

Mimica's mission is to empower enterprises, teams, and individuals to reclaim their most precious resource — time and work more efficiently, with greater purpose and impact.

Our AI-powered task mining observes employee actions across the desktop and categorizes them into detailed process maps. Mimica’s process intelligence highlights inefficiencies, prioritizes improvements based on ROI, recommends the optimal technology for automation (RPA, intelligent document processing, GenAI), and provides a blueprint for building new automations and transforming work.

Visit our Career Page https://www.mimica.ai/careers to learn more.



YOUR MISSION

Mimica is growing fast, and so is the demand on our Platform Team. As a DevOps / Platform Engineer, you will join a lean but highly capable team that is the backbone of every engineering squad in the company.

We service the entire engineering organisation: keeping environments healthy, unblocking teams, and building the infrastructure that lets everyone else move faster. We’re looking to increase our capacity as demand for our services is growing fast.

On top of the day-to-day demand from other teams, we are taking on some of our most ambitious infrastructure projects yet: expanding to multi-region and single-tenancy environments, supporting BYOC (Bring Your Own Cloud) deployments, migrating our IaC from Terraform to Crossplane, and getting serious about FinOps. This means the work is varied: one day you are debugging an RBAC issue in production, the next you are designing a multi-cloud access pattern from scratch.

We are looking for engineers with either an application development or a systems/infrastructure background.

What matters more than your origin story is your hands-on comfort with Kubernetes, IaC, and cloud platforms, your curiosity, and your ability to collaborate closely with other engineering teams. If you want to work in a place where your contributions are visible, your scope is broad, and the problems are real, this is the role for you.




PART OF YOUR DAY-TO-DAY

 - Support engineering teams across Mimica in developing mature, resilient applications running on GKE, advising on best practices and unblocking issues quickly.

 - Develop and maintain infrastructure-as-code using Terraform and ArgoCD, as well as Mimica-specific Platform applications written in Python.

 - Manage and maintain GKE environments across multiple regions (EKS experience is also acceptable), including IAM and identity management on GCP (AWS acceptable).

 - Investigate and triage issues in existing applications, observe database and system component health, and implement changes to support the SDLC of other teams.

 - Contribute to migrating our IaC layer from Terraform to Crossplane and support expansion into multi-region clusters and single-tenancy environments.

 - Drive FinOps initiatives, helping us get visibility into and control over cloud spend across multiple accounts and environments.

 - Help design and implement access patterns for new BYOC and single-tenancy environments, making it seamless for engineers to navigate an increasingly complex multi-environment landscape.

 - Instrument, monitor, and improve observability across our stack using tools such as Honeycomb and Grafana.

 


REQUIREMENTS / WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Solid hands-on experience with Kubernetes, whether on GKE (preferred), EKS, OnPrem, or another managed Kubernetes service. You know your way around cluster configuration, scheduling, RBAC, and debugging live issues.

 - Proven experience with Terraform as your primary IaC tool. Familiarity with Terragrunt is a plus.

 - IAM and identity management experience on at least one major hyperscaler: GCP (preferred) or AWS.

 - Experience with ArgoCD or a comparable GitOps tool for continuous delivery.

 - Observability experience with at least one major platform: Honeycomb, Datadog, Grafana, or New Relic. You know how to set up meaningful dashboards,

source_jobs.title: Head of/VP of Engineering
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE ARE BUILDING

Mimica's mission is to empower enterprises, teams, and individuals to reclaim their most precious resource — time and work more efficiently, with greater purpose and impact.

Our AI-powered task mining observes employee actions across the desktop and categorizes them into detailed process maps. Mimica’s process intelligence highlights inefficiencies, prioritizes improvements based on ROI, recommends the optimal technology for automation (RPA, intelligent document processing, GenAI), and provides a blueprint for building new automations and transforming work.

Visit our Career Page https://www.mimica.ai/careers to learn more.







WHERE WE ARE IN OUR SCALE-UP JOURNEY

We’ve achieved a solid product-market fit and are now scaling with intention. You’ll join a 50-strong team of Senior+ engineers and Product leaders who prioritise product impact over mere headcount. We have 8 cross-functional teams, including a dedicated Platform team focused on infra & developer experience. We started building proprietary ML before the LLM boom, so we don't just consume models - we train them from the ground up.



 


OUR APPROACH TO ENGINEERING

 - We prioritize customer needs first

 - We work in small, cross-functional, feature-oriented teams

 - We have flexibility in terms of the problems we work on

 - We own the full lifecycle of our projects

 - We avoid silos and encourage taking up tasks in new areas

 - We balance quality and velocity

 - We have a shared responsibility for our production code

 - We each set our own routine to maximize our productivity
   
   




YOUR MISSION



You will be the first Head of Engineering at Mimica, leading our engineering organisation through its next major growth phase. With a recently closed Series B round and strong market traction, we are doubling the Product, Design & Engineering organisation from 50 to 100 over the coming year.

This role exists to evolve engineering into a mature, high-velocity organisation capable of supporting 10x growth in usage. You will define the technical vision and North Star, introduce scalable processes and standards, and partner closely with the CTO, Product, and ML leaders — all while preserving our strong culture of ownership, psychological safety, and servant leadership.

This is a high-impact opportunity to own engineering strategy at a well-funded AI company at an exciting growth stage. Your impact will be direct: shaping how fast and reliably we can ship Enterprise-grade software, and build on a foundation that allows Engineers to do their best work.

If you have experience scaling such an engineering organisation, we'd love to chat!




PART OF YOUR DAY-TO-DAY

 

 1. Introduce scalable engineering processes and practices while preserving a culture of strong ownership, psychological safety, individual initiative, a supportive environment, and servant leadership.

 2. Define and evolve the Engineering Strategy and North Star to support 10x usage growth. Set a clear technical vision and roadmaps that align workstreams, technical standards, and business goals (e.g. balancing speed versus quality).

 3. Facilitate organisation-wide engineering planning, factoring in the prioritisation set by each team, and advance the necessary investments (headcount, vendors, infrastructure) to accommodate needs and long-term goals.

 4. Partner with the CTO, function leaders (Product, ML, Sales, People) to develop a strong product, business and talent strategy.

 5. Align hiring strategy with organisational goals by improving procedures, forecasting talent needs, and improving our value proposition to attract and retain top talent.

 6. Improve cross-team collaboration by preventing silos, duplicate work, and supporting coordination with Product while maintaining transparency and open-door policies.

 7. Drive the development and enablement of Engineering Managers through regular 1:1s, coaching, and career progression support. Build and maintain career l

source_jobs.title: Account Executive, Mid-Market
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE ARE BUILDING

Mimica's mission is to empower enterprises, teams, and individuals to reclaim their most precious resource — time and work more efficiently, with greater purpose and impact.

Our AI-powered task mining observes employee actions across the desktop and categorizes them into detailed process maps. Mimica’s process intelligence highlights inefficiencies, prioritizes improvements based on ROI, recommends the optimal technology for automation (RPA, intelligent document processing, GenAI), and provides a blueprint for building new automations and transforming work.

Visit our Career Page https://www.mimica.ai/careers to learn more.



YOUR MISSION

As a Mid-Market Account Executive at Mimica, you will play a critical role in scaling our market share by identifying, engaging, and converting high-potential accounts. You’ll operate at the intersection of outbound-driven pipeline generation and a scaling inbound engine, turning opportunity into revenue through disciplined execution.




PART OF YOUR DAY-TO-DAY

 - Identify, develop, and close new business opportunities across a defined portfolio of mid-market accounts.

 - Own and execute the full sales cycle, from prospecting and qualification through to negotiation and close.

 - Partner closely with BDRs, Solutions Engineers, and marketing to drive coordinated account engagement.

 - Build and expand relationships across multiple stakeholders, including senior decision-makers, to drive deal progression.

 - Understand the strategic and operational priorities of your target accounts and position Mimica’s value accordingly.

 - Lead structured, multi-threaded sales processes, navigating complex buying groups and procurement requirements.

 - Deliver tailored demos and business cases aligned to customer use cases.

 - Continuously refine your approach based on market feedback and performance data.




WHAT YOU WILL BRING

 - Demonstrated success in SaaS sales, with a strong focus on new business acquisition (“hunter” mentality).

 - Proven ability to manage and close complex deals involving multiple stakeholders.

 - Experience owning the full sales cycle, including pipeline generation and deal execution.

 - Track record of consistent quota attainment in mid-market or comparable segments.

 - Ability to operate effectively in low-brand or emerging market environments.

 - Strong commercial acumen and ability to articulate value in a clear, compelling way.

 - High level of ownership, urgency, and adaptability in fast-paced environments.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - Consistent generation of a high-quality pipeline across your account portfolio.

 - Strong execution of multi-stakeholder, 6-figure sales cycles.

 - Meaningful contribution to Mimica’s mid-market revenue growth.




WHAT WE OFFER

💰 Generous compensation + stock options - aligned with our internal framework, market data, and individual skills.

🏢 Distributed work: Work from anywhere - fully remote, in our hubs, or a mix.

💻 Company-issued laptop, remote setup stipend, and co-working budget

📍 Flexible schedules and location

☀️ Ample paid time off, in addition to local public holidays

🍼 Enhanced parental leave

🧘‍♀️ Health & retirement benefits

📖 Annual learning & development budget

🌴 Annual workaways and regular virtual & in-person socials

🌍 Opportunity to contribute to groundbreaking projects that shape the future of work



Note: Some benefits may vary depending on location and role



⚠️ Mimica will only contact candidates from an @mimica.ai email address. We do not request banking or sensitive personal information during the recruiting process.

source_jobs.title: Senior Solutions Engineer (Pre-Sales)
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE ARE BUILDING

Mimica's mission is to empower enterprises, teams, and individuals to reclaim their most precious resource — time and work more efficiently, with greater purpose and impact.

Our AI-powered task mining observes employee actions across the desktop and categorizes them into detailed process maps. Mimica’s process intelligence highlights inefficiencies, prioritizes improvements based on ROI, recommends the optimal technology for automation (RPA, intelligent document processing, GenAI), and provides a blueprint for building new automations and transforming work.

Visit our Career Page https://www.mimica.ai/careers to learn more.





PART OF YOUR DAY-TO-DAY

 - Leading demos and supporting technical evaluations for the Mimica platform, ensuring a path to value realization and positive ROI.

 - Building and executing POC plans with success criteria, managing scopes, expectations, and timelines, and acting as the point of contact for questions, issues, or escalations.

 - Serving as the Voice of the Customer, providing insights to engineering and product teams and input for improvements and new feature development.

 - Contributing to product documentation by adding clear explanations, examples, and how-to guides.

 - Partnering with Marketing to deliver educational content such as blog posts, tutorials, and presentations.

 - Assisting BDRs in identifying compelling use cases and tailoring messaging to value drivers specific to each industry, vertical, or function.

 - Nurturing strong relationships with key decision-makers at all levels, turning them into advocates and champions.

 - Acting as a trusted advisor for customers, deepening relationships and driving account growth including renewals and upsells.




WHAT YOU WILL BRING

 - Background in Solution Architecture, Sales Engineering or Implementation/Management Consulting, shaping solutions that add strategic and quantitative value.

 - Experience in devising and delivering complex enterprise software/SaaS solutions, tying them to customers’ business initiatives, pain points and long-term roadmaps.

 - Track record of successfully scoping, managing and executing pilots and production implementations, overcoming objections and removing friction in the pre-sales process.

 - Superior communication skills with the ability to switch between being deep in the weeds and taking the 10,000-foot view when presenting to stakeholders.

 - Empathy and product intuition – absorbing and interpreting feedback and working through and with others across functions to continually evolve Mimica’s capabilities.

 - Desire to continuously learn and improve your knowledge of our platform, our differentiating value and advancements in the Intelligent Automation domain.




BONUS

 - Knowledge of AI, task mining, process transformation or related markets.

 - Experience in successfully introducing B2B products to the market and/or new verticals.

 - Comfortable working within high-growth environments – delivering value quickly and iteratively.
   
   




WHAT WE OFFER

💰 Generous compensation + stock options - aligned with our internal framework, market data, and individual skills.

🏢 Distributed work: Work from anywhere - fully remote, in our hubs, or a mix.

💻 Company-issued laptop, remote setup stipend, and co-working budget

📍 Flexible schedules and location

☀️ Ample paid time off, in addition to local public holidays

🍼 Enhanced parental leave

🧘‍♀️ Health & retirement benefits

📖 Annual learning & development budget

🌴 Annual workaways and regular virtual & in-person socials

🌍 Opportunity to contribute to groundbreaking projects that shape the future of work



Note: Some benefits may vary depending on location and role



⚠️ Mimica will only contact candidates from an @mimica.ai email address. We do not request banking or sensitive personal information during the recruiting process.

source_jobs.title: Head of Partnerships
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE ARE BUILDING

Mimica's mission is to empower enterprises, teams, and individuals to reclaim their most precious resource — time and work more efficiently, with greater purpose and impact.

Our AI-powered task mining observes employee actions across the desktop and categorizes them into detailed process maps. Mimica’s process intelligence highlights inefficiencies, prioritizes improvements based on ROI, recommends the optimal technology for automation (RPA, intelligent document processing, GenAI), and provides a blueprint for building new automations and transforming work.

Visit our Career Page https://www.mimica.ai/careers to learn more.



YOUR MISSION

The primary goal of this role is to build and scale Mimica’s partner ecosystem into a meaningful revenue driver.

As Head of Partnerships, you will define and execute the strategy to expand market reach through System Integrators, MSPs, and technology partners. This role is central to how Mimica grows, embedding partners into our go-to-market motion to drive pipeline, accelerate deal cycles, and extend our enterprise footprint.




PART OF YOUR DAY-TO-DAY

 - Design and implement Mimica’s partner strategy across key channels (e.g., SIs, MSPs, ISVs).

 - Identify, recruit, and develop high-impact partners that expand distribution and unlock new revenue opportunities.

 - Define how partners engage across the sales cycle, including sourcing, co-selling, and delivery.

 - Build and strengthen executive-level relationships within partner organizations to drive long-term collaboration.

 - Collaborate closely with sales leadership to integrate partners into active deals and ensure alignment on incentives and execution.

 - Enable partners with clear positioning, use cases, and repeatable sales motions to drive pipeline and revenue.

 - Establish a scalable operating model, including performance metrics, pipeline tracking, and regular business reviews.

 - Continuously refine the partner approach based on performance, market dynamics, and revenue impact.
   
   


WHAT YOU WILL BRING

 - Proven experience building and scaling partnerships in B2B SaaS, consulting, or enterprise technology environments.

 - Track record of driving measurable revenue through partners, not just managing relationships.

 - Strong understanding of how System Integrators and consulting partners operate and monetize.

 - Experience working with complex enterprise sales cycles and multi-stakeholder environments.

 - Ability to design commercially sound partnership models that scale.

 - Strong cross-functional leadership, particularly with sales and go-to-market teams.

 - High level of ownership, autonomy, and comfort operating in ambiguous, fast-paced environments.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - A clearly defined and adopted partner model integrated into Mimica’s GTM motion.

 - Strong pipeline and revenue contribution from partner-sourced and partner-influenced deals.

 - Productive, scalable partnerships across key channels.

 - Consistent engagement between sales and partners across active opportunities.

 - A repeatable operating cadence with clear performance visibility and accountability.




WHAT WE OFFER

💰 Generous compensation + stock options - aligned with our internal framework, market data, and individual skills.

🏢 Distributed work: Work from anywhere - fully remote, in our hubs, or a mix.

💻 Company-issued laptop, remote setup stipend, and co-working budget

📍 Flexible schedules and location

☀️ Ample paid time off, in addition to local public holidays

🍼 Enhanced parental leave

🧘‍♀️ Health & retirement benefits

source_jobs.title: Staff/Lead Front-end Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE ARE BUILDING

Mimica's mission is to empower enterprises, teams, and individuals to reclaim their most precious resource — time and work more efficiently, with greater purpose and impact.

Our AI-powered task mining observes employee actions across the desktop and categorizes them into detailed process maps. Mimica’s process intelligence highlights inefficiencies, prioritizes improvements based on ROI, recommends the optimal technology for automation (RPA, intelligent document processing, GenAI), and provides a blueprint for building new automations and transforming work.

Visit our Career Page https://www.mimica.ai/careers to learn more.




Where we are in our scale-up journey

We’ve achieved strong product-market fit and are now scaling with intention. You’ll join a 50-person team of Senior+ engineers and Product leaders who prioritise product impact over mere headcount. We have 8 cross-functional teams, including a dedicated Platform team focused on infra & developer experience. We started building proprietary ML before the LLM boom, so we don't just consume models - we train them from the ground up.






Our approach to engineering

 - We prioritize customer needs first 

 - We work in small, cross-functional, feature-oriented teams

 - We have flexibility in terms of the problems we work on

 - We own the full lifecycle of our projects

 - We avoid silos and encourage taking up tasks in new areas

 - We balance quality and velocity

 - We have a shared responsibility for our production code

 - We each set our own routine to maximize our productivity







YOUR MISSION



You will join our Mapper Team, whose goal is to produce and service the Process Maps that are at the core of Mimica's product.

We found good product-market fit, which means two things: building new features and making architectural decisions that will enable exponential growth in usage.

You will take ownership of projects end-to-end, build new features, release improvements to existing components, and take operational responsibility for what you build.

We have a bottom-up culture where Engineers identify pain points, experiment with and iterate on their solutions, and leverage tight feedback loops.

As such, you‘ll have the opportunity to shape the technical direction, processes and culture of the Engineering org, Chapter and Team.









PART OF YOUR DAY-TO-DAY

 - Developing data-visualisation applications and new features around displaying significant large data properties (e.g., insights regarding processes, workflows and metrics) in effective ways, with scalability & reliability in mind.

 - Building interfaces and real-time frontend services that enable multiple users to edit graph data and re-train AI models through their input.

 - Owning projects end-to-end as part of small teams, collaborating with our TLs and Product Managers.

 - Participating in architectural decisions, collaborating across teams to identify and implement product improvements and producing documentation for your implementations.

 - Participate in and improve the Team's and Chapter's internal processes and rituals so that we're more effective.

 - Providing feedback to peers through RFCs, code reviews and helping those around you to level up.

 - Participating in hiring and onboarding new team members.







WHO YOU ARE

 - You have at least 8+ years of experience as a Software Engineer and high engineering standards.

 - Strong experience and understanding of React, state management, front-end architecture & concepts.

 - Deep understanding of Front-end performance optimisation techniques.

 - Knowledge of advanced system design principles, data structures, and the core concepts underlying data-intensive applications.

 - Minimum knowledge of Backend Engineering (eg. being able to add API endpoints, change logic)

 - Committed to upholding high engineering standards, delivering clean, testable, reusable, modular, and well-documented code.

 - Experience own

source_jobs.title: Associate Business Development Representative, NAMER
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE ARE BUILDING

Mimica's mission is to empower enterprises, teams, and individuals to reclaim their most precious resource — time and work more efficiently, with greater purpose and impact.

Our AI-powered task mining observes employee actions across the desktop and categorizes them into detailed process maps. Mimica’s process intelligence highlights inefficiencies, prioritizes improvements based on ROI, recommends the optimal technology for automation (RPA, intelligent document processing, GenAI), and provides a blueprint for building new automations and transforming work.

Visit our Career Page https://www.mimica.ai/careers to learn more.


Your Launchpad into Enterprise Sales

Kickstart your SaaS sales career at one of the fastest-growing startups in Process Intelligence and Automation. At Mimica, you’ll gain hands-on experience working with Fortune 500 companies, learn how to navigate complex organizations, and develop the skills to become a world-class enterprise seller. As part of our early sales team, you’ll fuel our growth by creating new business opportunities, engaging key decision-makers, and opening doors for our Enterprise Account Executives.

Part of your day-to-day

 - Build and prioritize target account lists to identify and create new business opportunities.

 - Craft and send personalized outreach — from cold call scripts to LinkedIn and email sequences — using tools like Outreach and LinkedIn Sales Navigator.

 - Execute outbound and inbound campaigns, following up with marketing-qualified leads to move prospects through the funnel.

 - Qualify prospects through discovery conversations and set up high-quality meetings for the Account Executives.

 - Join discovery calls and demos to observe how experienced sellers engage with senior stakeholders and learn how enterprise deals are developed.

 - Track pipeline activity and keep Salesforce up to date with details on accounts, deal size, and status to support accurate forecasting.

 - Collaborate with the team to refine sales processes, messaging, and tools as you grow into a full-cycle enterprise seller.

What you will bring

 - Demonstrated academic or internship track record that reflects competitiveness, initiative, and a strong work ethic — you go above and beyond to deliver results.

 - Commitment to a career in technical software sales — developing pipelines, prospecting, and qualifying leads.

 - Eagerness to learn and continually build on your knowledge of Mimica’s value proposition, market, and target personas.

 - Drive to achieve personal and career goals despite rejections or setbacks.

 - Discipline and the ability to apply a structured process in order to meet your quota consistently.

 - Superior verbal and written communication skills — you are comfortable initiating conversations, articulate, and not afraid to share a point of view, but also able to ask questions and actively listen.

 - Proven interest in AI, ML, or automation.




WHAT WE OFFER

💰 Generous compensation + stock options - aligned with our internal framework, market data, and individual skills.

🏢 Distributed work: Work from anywhere - fully remote, in our hubs, or a mix.

💻 Company-issued laptop, remote setup stipend, and co-working budget

📍 Flexible schedules and location

☀️ Ample paid time off, in addition to local public holidays

🍼 Enhanced parental leave

🧘‍♀️ Health & retirement benefits

📖 Annual learning & development budget

🌴 Annual workaways and regular virtual & in-person socials

🌍 Opportunity to contribute to groundbreaking projects that shape the future of work



Note: Some benefits may vary depending on location and role



⚠️ Mimica will only contact candidates from an @mimica.ai email address. We do not request banking or sensitive personal information during the recruiting process.

source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative, NAMER
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE ARE BUILDING

Mimica's mission is to empower enterprises, teams, and individuals to reclaim their most precious resource — time and work more efficiently, with greater purpose and impact.

Our AI-powered task mining observes employee actions across the desktop and categorizes them into detailed process maps. Mimica’s process intelligence highlights inefficiencies, prioritizes improvements based on ROI, recommends the optimal technology for automation (RPA, intelligent document processing, GenAI), and provides a blueprint for building new automations and transforming work.

Visit our Career Page https://www.mimica.ai/careers to learn more.


Elevate Your Sales Career

If you already have up to two years of experience in sales — whether that’s in SaaS, door-to-door, or another outbound selling environment — this is your opportunity to make the leap into AI. At Mimica, you’ll learn how to navigate complex buying processes, engage senior stakeholders, and master the art of multi-threaded, high-value selling. As part of our early sales team, you’ll fuel our growth by generating qualified opportunities, building relationships with key decision-makers, and laying the foundation for a long-term career in software sales.



Part of your day-to-day

 - Build and prioritize strategic target account lists to uncover and develop new business opportunities.

 - Craft and send personalized outreach — from cold call scripts to LinkedIn and email sequences — using tools like Outreach and LinkedIn Sales Navigator.

 - Execute outbound and inbound campaigns, following up with marketing-qualified leads to keep the pipeline active and growing.

 - Qualify and nurture opportunities through discovery conversations, setting up high-quality meetings for the Account Executives.

 - Track pipeline activity and maintain accurate, detailed records in Salesforce, including account info, deal size, and status, to support forecasting.

 - Collaborate with the broader sales team to continuously improve our playbooks, processes, and tools.
   
   

What you will bring

 - Drive to achieve personal and career goals despite rejections or setbacks.

 - Prior cloud/SaaS sales development experience — researching and building new and existing accounts.

 - Proven interest in AI, ML, or automation.

 - Familiarity working within growth-stage startup environments — delivering value quickly and iteratively.




WHAT WE OFFER

source_jobs.title: Staff/Lead Fullstack Engineer (Frontend-focused)
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE ARE BUILDING

Mimica's mission is to empower enterprises, teams, and individuals to reclaim their most precious resource — time and work more efficiently, with greater purpose and impact.

Our AI-powered task mining observes employee actions across the desktop and categorizes them into detailed process maps. Mimica’s process intelligence highlights inefficiencies, prioritizes improvements based on ROI, recommends the optimal technology for automation (RPA, intelligent document processing, GenAI), and provides a blueprint for building new automations and transforming work.

Visit our Career Page https://www.mimica.ai/careers to learn more.




Where we are in our scale-up journey

We’ve achieved strong product-market fit and are now scaling with intention. You’ll join a 50-person team of Senior+ engineers and Product leaders who prioritise product impact over mere headcount. We have 8 cross-functional teams, including a dedicated Platform team focused on infra & developer experience. We started building proprietary ML before the LLM boom, so we don't just consume models - we train them from the ground up.






Our approach to engineering

 - We prioritize customer needs first 

 - We work in small, cross-functional, feature-oriented teams

 - We have flexibility in terms of the problems we work on

 - We own the full lifecycle of our projects

 - We avoid silos and encourage taking up tasks in new areas

 - We balance quality and velocity

 - We have a shared responsibility for our production code

 - We each set our own routine to maximize our productivity







YOUR MISSION



You will join our Mapper Team, whose goal is to produce and service the Process Maps that are at the core of Mimica's product.

We found good product-market fit, which means two things: building new features and making architectural decisions that will enable exponential growth in usage.

You will take ownership of projects end-to-end, build new features, release improvements to existing components, and take operational responsibility for what you build.

We have a bottom-up culture where Engineers identify pain points, experiment with and iterate on their solutions, and leverage tight feedback loops.

As such, you‘ll have the opportunity to shape the technical direction, processes and culture of the Engineering org, Chapter and Team.









PART OF YOUR DAY-TO-DAY

 - Developing data-visualisation applications and new features around displaying significant large data properties (e.g., insights regarding processes, workflows and metrics) in effective ways, with scalability & reliability in mind.

 - Building interfaces and real-time frontend services that enable multiple users to edit graph data and re-train AI models through their input.

 - Owning projects end-to-end as part of small teams, collaborating with our TLs and Product Managers.

 - Participating in architectural decisions, collaborating across teams to identify and implement product improvements and producing documentation for your implementations.

 - Participate in and improve the Team's and Chapter's internal processes and rituals so that we're more effective.

 - Providing feedback to peers through RFCs, code reviews and helping those around you to level up.

 - Participating in hiring and onboarding new team members.







WHO YOU ARE

 - You have at least 8+ years of experience as a Software Engineer and high engineering standards.

 - Strong experience and understanding of React, state management, front-end architecture & concepts.

 - Knowledge of advanced system design principles, data structures, algorithms, and the core concepts underlying data-intensive applications.

 - Deep understanding of advanced performance optimisation techniques.

 - Previous experience working in the backend.

 - Committed to upholding high engineering standards, delivering clean, testable, reusable, modular, and well-documented code.

 - Experience owning projects from start to finish, inc

source_jobs.title: Staff/Lead Python Engineer (FastAPI, Orchestration)
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE ARE BUILDING

Mimica's mission is to empower enterprises, teams, and individuals to reclaim their most precious resource — time and work more efficiently, with greater purpose and impact.

Our AI-powered task mining observes employee actions across the desktop and categorizes them into detailed process maps. Mimica’s process intelligence highlights inefficiencies, prioritizes improvements based on ROI, recommends the optimal technology for automation (RPA, intelligent document processing, GenAI), and provides a blueprint for building new automations and transforming work.

Visit our Career Page https://www.mimica.ai/careers to learn more.





WHERE WE ARE IN OUR SCALE-UP JOURNEY

We’ve achieved strong product-market fit and are now scaling with intention. You’ll join a 45-person team of Senior+ engineers and Product leaders who prioritise product impact over mere headcount. We have 8 cross-functional teams, including a dedicated Platform team focused on infra & developer experience. We started building proprietary ML before the LLM boom, so we don't just consume models - we train them from the ground up.



Our approach to engineering

 - We prioritise customer needs first

 - We work in small, project-based teams

 - We have flexibility in terms of the problems we work on

 - We own the full lifecycle of our projects

 - We avoid silos and encourage taking up tasks in new areas

 - We balance quality and velocity

 - We have a shared responsibility for our production code

 - We each set our own routine to maximise our productivity




WHAT WILL YOU OWN



In this role, you will own and support the development of backend systems that interface with ML models. You will build apps and core components of our ML systems, deliver new AI features and drive improvements to our infrastructure and services.

We are currently looking for Engineers to join one of our teams:

 - Data Intelligence Team, whose goal is to provide ML-enriched data for downstream tasks and ensure user data privacy. You will support our data orchestration and processing pipeline, as we build with scalability in mind.

 - Miner Team, whose goal is to enable rapid and accurate labelling and segmentation of data in the Miner product through automation and ML methods. You will support the automation and scalability of the existing ML systems that back up our Miner product.

 - Maker Team, a team responsible for building out the automation engine, as one of its founding members. We're building a new product from scratch, and that means being a part of the design of its architecture, experimentation and development.



In all cases, we're looking for a Lead/Staff-level engineer to join the Python Chapter, who will have the opportunity to shape our technical direction, architecture, processes, and culture, and to level up the team and Chapter in development practices.




WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING



 - Write Python applications that are resilient, robust, and integrate well with other apps in a service architecture.

 - Furthering Developer Experience (DevEx) by mentoring others in writing code that is intuitive, clear, and easy to test

 - Developing observability for new and existing ML applications and GenAI/LLM integrations, making use of the Grafana Stack (Prometheus, Loki, Tempo)

 - Develop integrations and services that communicate with Google Services.

 - Working closely with Data Scientists and ML Engineers throughout the lifecycle of productionising their models

 - Being responsive to incidents regarding ML applications - including an understanding of how to triage and resolve issues relating to CPU, memory, and GPU utilisation

 - Documenting procedures and guides to facilitate knowledge sharing and help other engineers level up through pairing and mentoring

 - Participating in hiring and onboarding new team members; taking on end-to-end project management responsibilities as we grow.

 - At least 8 years of

source_jobs.title: Founding Demand Generation Lead
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE ARE BUILDING

Mimica's mission is to empower enterprises, teams, and individuals to reclaim their most precious resource — time and work more efficiently, with greater purpose and impact.

Our AI-powered task mining observes employee actions across the desktop and categorizes them into detailed process maps. Mimica’s process intelligence highlights inefficiencies, prioritizes improvements based on ROI, recommends the optimal technology for automation (RPA, intelligent document processing, GenAI), and provides a blueprint for building new automations and transforming work.

Visit our Career Page https://www.mimica.ai/careers to learn more.



YOUR MISSION

As a Demand Generation Manager, you will play a critical role in expanding market presence by driving high-quality pipeline through a combination of inbound and marketing-led initiatives. You will shape how enterprise buyers discover and engage with Mimica, ensuring that demand generation efforts translate into measurable revenue impact.




PART OF YOUR DAY-TO-DAY

 - Build and scale a digital-first demand generation engine that drives qualified enterprise pipeline.

 - Design and execute multi-channel campaigns across paid media, email, SEO, and social, aligned to target segments and markets.

 - Develop and balance broad-based demand generation initiatives with targeted, account-focused programs.

 - Continuously test, measure, and refine messaging and campaign performance based on data and sales feedback.

 - Partner closely with sales to ensure strong alignment on pipeline quality, conversion, and follow-through.

 - Own funnel performance across key stages, including pipeline generation, conversion rates, and campaign ROI.

 - Drive the promotion and distribution of key initiatives (e.g. content, webinars, campaigns) to maximize reach and impact.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to ensure consistent messaging and execution across the go-to-market motion.


WHAT YOU WILL BRING

 - Proven experience in B2B SaaS demand generation with direct ownership of pipeline creation.

 - Strong track record of building and scaling digital-first campaigns that generate qualified pipeline.

 - Ability to translate market insights and performance data into messaging and campaign optimization.

 - Experience operating across both strategic planning and hands-on execution.

 - Strong understanding of paid channels (e.g. LinkedIn, Google), SEO, email marketing, and conversion optimization.

 - Analytical mindset with experience in attribution, experimentation, and funnel performance management.

 - High level of ownership and adaptability in fast-paced, high-growth environments.




WHAT WE OFFER

💰 Generous compensation + stock options - aligned with our internal framework, market data, and individual skills.

🏢 Distributed work: Work from anywhere - fully remote, in our hubs, or a mix.

💻 Company-issued laptop, remote setup stipend, and co-working budget

📍 Flexible schedules and location

☀️ Ample paid time off, in addition to local public holidays

🍼 Enhanced parental leave

🧘‍♀️ Health & retirement benefits

📖 Annual learning & development budget

🌴 Annual workaways and regular virtual & in-person socials

🌍 Opportunity to contribute to groundbreaking projects that shape the future of work



Note: Some benefits may vary depending on location and role



⚠️ Mimica will only contact candidates from an @mimica.ai email address. We do not request banking or sensitive personal information during the recruiting process.

 - Serve as a strategic advisor and thought leader in leveraging Mimica to achieve customers’ desired business outcomes.

 - Collaborate closely with Sales, Marketing, Solution Engineering and Partner teams to co-create and execute customer account strategies. Identify opportunities for upselling, cross-selling, and further expansion within existing accounts.

source_jobs.title: Senior HR/Employee Lifecycle Ops Specialist
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE ARE BUILDING

Mimica's mission is to empower enterprises, teams, and individuals to reclaim their most precious resource — time and work more efficiently, with greater purpose and impact.

Our AI-powered task mining observes employee actions across the desktop and categorizes them into detailed process maps. Mimica’s process intelligence highlights inefficiencies, prioritizes improvements based on ROI, recommends the optimal technology for automation (RPA, intelligent document processing, GenAI), and provides a blueprint for building new automations and transforming work.

Visit our Career Page https://www.mimica.ai/careers to learn more.



YOUR MISSION

We’re transitioning hundreds of employees in Costa Rica from EOR to a fully owned local entity. This is a high-impact operational shift. If payroll, benefits, or compliance obligations are wrong or missed, employees will feel it immediately. Your role will be to own the execution and integrity of this system — keeping it running cleanly, compliantly, and at scale.

You’ll go from cleaning and validating payroll inputs to owning statutory compliance, to shaping how we operate locally as we scale, with the potential to grow into leading all People Operations & HR in the region.




WHAT YOU WILL WORK ON

 - Own pre-hire compliance requirements, including right to work checks, documentation, and contract readiness, ensuring employees are correctly set up before day 1

 - Handle new joiner, leaver, and employment change transactions – generating contracts, amendments, employment verifications, salary certificates, and formal notices

 - Prepare, validate, and submit payroll inputs, including salary, incentives, deductions, and adjustments

 - Coordinate operational workflows across vendors and internal stakeholders to ensure payroll, benefits, and compliance processes run accurately and on schedule

 - Own monthly cutoffs, approvals, and reconciliations, ensuring dependencies are tracked and resolved proactively

 - Own enrolment, changes, and issue resolution for private healthcare and statutory benefits

 - Manage leave processes, including statutory and company leave, ensuring alignment across policy, systems, and payroll outputs

 - Be the go-to person for employee queries on employment terms, payslips, taxes, and benefits, resolving issues with clarity and confidence

 - Stay ahead of regulatory changes and implement them proactively, ensuring our practices are compliant with CCSS, MTSS, DGT, and INS

 - Support audits, filings, and statutory reporting for Costa Rica

 - Maintain clean, structured employee data across HRIS and payroll systems
   
   


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - Payroll runs accurately and on time without last-minute corrections

 - Compliance risks are identified and resolved before they become liabilities

 - Employee queries are handled quickly, clearly, and correctly

 - Data across systems is clean, consistent, and audit-ready

 - Employee lifecycle operations run smoothly without constant escalation
   
   


WHAT YOU WILL BRING

 - Background in HR services roles, with a solid understanding of cooridnating employee lifecycle operations, legal compliance and payroll

 - Working knowledge of labor law and employment practices in Costa Rica, and the ability to apply these in practice

 - Hands-on experience with contracts, time & attendance and benefits management, including how they impact payroll and compliance

 - Familiarity with complex, multi-entity environments across North, South America, and EMEA

 - Experience working through audits and actively supporting statutory reporting

 - Execution depth. You are comfortable doing detailed, repetitive work such as data input, audits, and reconciliation, and you consistently catch issues others miss

 - Proven ability to operate without full structure, interpreting requirements and building practical, fit-for-purpose processes as you go




WHAT WE OFFER

💰 Generous compensation + stock options

notes: High-tech AI-powered task mining company with strong engineering focus
